title: search: allow wildcards
body:
Allow trailing wildcards in the search term.  Note that not all
registries support wildcards and it may only work with v1 registries.
Hence, we just strip `*` before parsing the domain of the input.

Fixes: bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1846629
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@mtrmac @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat  @QiWang19 PTAL

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@baude @mheon this would be a nice to have for v2, assuming this PR is the right approach.
Some of the tests don't look happy.

> parse it manually, using whatever the semantics is 

That is the crux. I don't see a way to support arbitrary input. Especially with the search-registries. We need to extract a the domain/registry from the input, but if we support arbitrary input we just can't do that.

If we limit wildcard-support to trailing ones, we can keep the current parsing logic and support most of the use cases (at least all in the BZ).
Marked as WIP until we figured out if trailing-only would meet the customer demands.

> > parse it manually, using whatever the semantics is
> 
> That is the crux. I don't see a way to support arbitrary input. Especially with the search-registries. We need to extract a the domain/registry from the input, but if we support arbitrary input we just can't do that.

I didn’t mean “whatever the semantics of the remote registry wildcards is”, just “whatever the semantics of the argument to `podman search` is”.

Looking at https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/88ee5d4f5f8ba6afabbb921db7b0ba7e92d59148/libpod/image/search.go#L71-L78 , we parse the input string into (`registry`, `term`), and only `term` is passed to the registry’s API (and thus has unknown semantics).

The semantics of the split into registry+term is entirely within Podman’s control. Isn’t what we want basically a “registry is the part before the first `/` if any”? I suppose we should have unit tests for all the affected cases, but I don’t think it’s much more than that.
Brilliant idea to consider a registry before `/`, @mtrmac!

Looking into this a bit further, the tests of `reference.Parse` do suggest that `foo/bar` is parsed as “domain == `foo`”.

OTOH there is a difference in error handling/format enforcement, for inputs like 'what#is/this\even'.

> Looking into this a bit further, the tests of `reference.Parse` do suggest that `foo/bar` is parsed as “domain == `foo`”.
> 
> OTOH there is a difference in error handling/format enforcement, for inputs like 'what#is/this\even'.

The podman-search behaviour is quite curious in some cases already:
```
~ $ podman search registry.redhat.io
INDEX       NAME                                               DESCRIPTION                                       STARS   OFFICIAL   AUTOMATED
docker.io   docker.io/knqyf263/registry.redhat.io-ubi7                                                           0                  
docker.io   docker.io/abdennour/rhel                           RHEL based on registry.redhat.io/ubi8/ubi-in...   0                  
docker.io   docker.io/chengkuan/amq-streams-kafka-connect-23   Kafka MongoDB Connect based on registry.redh...   0                  
```

It really is quite challenging for parsing. Docker has such a nice life as it only normalizes to docker.io.
Rebased on master to unblock CI
